entry.title: Re: Which products are addressed in the SAP Activate methodology for the Intelligent Enterprise road
entry.content:
A) Extended package
C) Baseline package

It's quite easy to find correct answers, Google is your friend 🙂

To facilitate the adoption of SAP Cloud products, several services and tools are available. One example is the Strategic Advisory Service for Analytics.
The service is offered in three scope options:

  • Baseline Package: This introductory option socializes in-depth advisory services with the customer. In a quick assessment, high-level opportunities and quick wins are identified.

  • Enhanced package: This intermediate service initiates in-depth analytics with a transformation road map. The package includes a baseline service scope, value proposition, analytics capability formulation, and recommendations for governance, standards, architecture, and project execution.

  • Extended package: This enhanced service scope provides ongoing support. A dedicated analytics advisor is assigned as the central contact for all discussions about analytics solutions from SAP, and manages related initiatives to deliver business value for medium- and long-term execution.

entry.title: Re: How to solve simple requirement with RAP, Fiori Eleme...
entry.content:
Dear Patrick

Thank you for taking the time to answer to my post. I appreciate it.

Review of your solution:

Acutally my first attempt was a lot like yours with pure Fiori Elements and Preview in ADT, only I used V4 GUI (wonder why you used V2?). 

I also used "fake" internal create, update, delete operations on a "fake" Employee Table (in your case zpw_empl) in the behaviour defintion, as it is a must that the root entity (Employee) is defined in the behaviour definiton. 

What I miss in your solution is the business requirement to have a list of employees first  from a HCM System (Tables PAxxxx, SAP delivered or Custom CDS does not matter), where you can filter and select an EXISTING employee and only then the object page with a list of uploaded documents for that selected employee. Instead you display just a list of uploaded documents with associated emplyoee data from a table/cds (zpw_empl) that does not exist in HCM. Thus in your solution there will be no entry in the list, if the employee has no documents. 

Please see my attached screenshot (Fiori Elements with ADT Preview of RAP with V4 GUI).  And then the odyssey began!

I will of course try out your V2 solution (with my specific requirements) and let you know how this worked out.

Kind regards
Paul

PS:
To complete this real life business case I have to 2 more requirements (among others)
which give me a headache with RAP, Fiori Elements and FPM:

1) I need a value help on the field "LZ" in the List (see attachement) which will
    display a "hierarchy" (there are many hierarchies in FI und HCM).
    If the End User selects a "father" Node (LZ) of this hierarchy, the list of employees
    must show all employees of the father node (if any) including those who belong to a
    "child" Nodes. 
2) Depending on the user starting up the App the list of employees will always be
    filtered according to this "structural" Authorization (hint: in HCM HRP1001),
    this is again a Hierarchy. If the user is defined as "HR Document Administrator" to a      node, he is able to add, delete, create document for users belonging to that node
    (and child  nodes). Can't be solved with standard access control.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Seeking Support to Achieve My Dream: SAP HCM Associate Certificate

As an aspiring SAP HCM professional, I am on a journey to deepen my skills and knowledge in the world of SAP. Over the past year, I have transitioned from an SAP ABAP developer role into an SAP HCM consultant position. This shift has allowed me to explore the intricate workings of Human Capital Management and develop hands-on experience with SAP's comprehensive solutions.

In my current role, I have gained valuable experience in understanding the complexities of SAP HCM, such as personnel administration, organizational management, and time management. However, to further my professional growth and truly solidify my foundation, I am keen on earning the SAP HCM Associate Certification. Achieving this certification would not only validate my skills but also open new opportunities for career advancement and allow me to contribute more effectively to my organization.

Unfortunately, due to financial constraints, I am unable to afford the cost of the certification. I am reaching out to the SAP community and certification bodies to seek support for obtaining this valuable credential. I believe that with a certification, I can maximize my potential and continue contributing to SAP HCM projects with confidence and expertise.

Why Am I a Worthy Candidate?

  • Passion for SAP HCM: I am passionate about leveraging SAP technology to optimize HR processes and improve organizational efficiency. I consistently invest time in self-learning through available online resources, webinars, and hands-on practice.

  • Practical Experience: With my background as an SAP ABAP developer and my current role as an SAP HCM consultant, I have acquired a blend of technical and functional skills. This combination enables me to approach SAP HCM problems with a holistic perspective, bridging the gap between technical development and business requirements.

  • Commitment to Professional Growth: Despite financial challenges, I remain dedicated to advancing my career and skill set. The SAP HCM certification would serve as a catalyst for my professional development and would enable me to deliver better outcomes for clients and projects.

How Can SAP and the Community Support Me?

I am requesting assistance in the form of a scholarship or sponsorship to cover the certification cost. By investing in me, SAP or any supporting organization would not only be helping an individual achieve their goals but also contributing to the growth of the SAP community by enabling a committed professional to flourish.

Thank you for taking the time to read my story. I remain hopeful that I will find a way to achieve this certification and continue my journey as an SAP HCM consultant. Your support would be greatly appreciated, and I promise to pay it forward by helping others in similar situations once I am in a position to do so.

Sincerely,
Yeswanthraj Krishnan.

entry.title: Re: Show more than one currency in the same planning view
entry.content:
Hi,

Exchange rate key figure used in calculation is conversion enabled. Please try if below works

Create a new attribute as key figure in Exchange rate MDT or a new key figure Exchange Rate at MTHCURRCURRTO1 planning level. Make sure MTHCURRCURRTO1 planning level doesn't have conversion source & conversion target marked. Then use this new key figure in the calculation where you want to display both target currency.

Regards,

Piyush

entry.title: Tech Day: dive into the latest innovations in SAP BTP, SAC, Datasphere and more
entry.content:
Who are we?

We are a group of motivated developers and data experts, passionate about SAP, BTP, analytics and developing awesome applications! With this event we want to bring together people with a similar mindset, looking to explore SAP’s latest offerings and gain new insights.

 

What to expect?

  • Arrive at 12:30 PM for lunch and meet your fellow attendees.
  • At 1:30 PM we’ll kick off the afternoon with a keynote that will inspire you for the rest of the program.
  • After the keynote, we’ll split up in different tracks: Analytics & Integration, Extensions, and a Trending track. Have a look at the complete program here.
  • At 3:00 PM we'll hold a sweet break where you can talk with the experts.
  • At around 5:45 PM we'll call it a day, grab a drink (you can even participate in a beer tasting) and a bite and start connecting.

 

Practical information

Date: November 7, from 12:30 till 19:30

Location: Palm Brewery (De Hoorn) | Steenhuffeldorp 3, 1840 Londerzeel

 

Who can attend?

Flexso Tech Day is free to attend for every SAP developer and BI expert looking to stay up to date with the latest SAP technologies and network with like-minded spirits. Places are limited though, so register now and be sure of your spot!

 

We hope to see you there!

entry.title: Re: Add action button on Custom Business Object exposed a...
entry.content:
Hello!

I am unsure with your version if you can call the BAPI directly inside the Implementation method of your action, implemented in the Behaviour Implementation Class. 

If not, you will have to create a Class and method to call the BAPI and expose it as C1 to call it inside your RAP Behaviour Implementation.

The only tricky thing is the commit that you need to perform. For that, we came up with a workaround by wrapping the BAPI in a custom RFC function module, similar to this (this is for Planned Order creation but the logic is the same):

CALL FUNCTION 'ZPP_FM_CUTSIZE_CREATE_ORDER'

STARTING NEW TASK 'Z_ORDER'

DESTINATION 'NONE'

CALLING response_action ON END OF TASK

EXPORTING

logical_system = mv_logsys

order_type = '5'

ext_number_assignment = 'X'

commit_control = 'E' "E

strategy_profile = ls_strategy_profile

event_control = '1'

planning_mode_usage = '1'

TABLES

order_head = lt_order_head

order_structure = lt_order_structure

order_components = lt_order_components

characteristics_val_io = lt_characteristics_val_io

order_modes = lt_o_mode

mapping_data = lt_mapping_data

return = lt_ret

extension_in = lt_extension_in

CHANGING

cv_complete = lv_is_complete

EXCEPTIONS

communication_failure = 1

system_failure = 2

OTHERS = 3.

WAIT UNTIL lv_is_complete EQ abap_true.

response action method to receive the result:

METHOD response_action.

 

DATA lt_mapping_data TYPE TABLE OF bapi10503ordmapreto.

 

RECEIVE RESULTS FROM FUNCTION 'ZPP_FM_CREATE_ORDER'

IMPORTING return = mt_bapi_create_ret

mapping_data = lt_mapping_data .

 

READ TABLE lt_mapping_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_mapping_data>) INDEX 1.

CHECK <fs_mapping_data> IS ASSIGNED.

mv_planned_order = <fs_mapping_data>-order_number.

 

 

ENDMETHOD.



code inside ZPP_FM_CREATE_ORDER:

 

 

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_MOSRVAPS_SAVETMPLPPM' DESTINATION 'NONE'

EXPORTING

logical_system = logical_system

order_type = order_type

ext_number_assignment = ext_number_assignment

commit_control = commit_control

strategy_profile = strategy_profile

planning_mode_usage = planning_mode_usage

event_control = event_control

TABLES

order_head = order_head

order_structure = order_structure

order_components = order_components

characteristics_val_io = characteristics_val_io

characteristics_val_act = characteristics_val_act

order_modes = order_modes

mapping_data = mapping_data

return = return

extension_in = extension_in

EXCEPTIONS

communication_failure = 1

system_failure = 2

OTHERS = 3.

 

IF sy-subrc NE 0.

DATA ls_ret TYPE bapiret2.

MOVE sy-msgid TO ls_ret-id.

MOVE sy-msgno TO ls_ret-number.

MOVE sy-msgty TO ls_ret-type.

MOVE sy-msgv1 TO ls_ret-message_v1.

MOVE sy-msgv2 TO ls_ret-message_v2.

MOVE sy-msgv3 TO ls_ret-message_v3.

MOVE sy-msgv4 TO ls_ret-message_v4.

 

MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno INTO ls_ret-message

WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.

APPEND ls_ret TO return.

ENDIF.

 

cv_complete = abap_true.

 

 

Let me know if this helps you in any case. We have implemented it and it works well. I can try to help if you need further help 🙂

Case 1

25/07 First payment : 19128, 73 ( Qty of 382 for unit price of 42.08)

13/08 Second payment : 2822.94 (Qty of 382 for unit price of 48.29)

entry.title: Fallback logical port for MDG BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateConfirmation_Out is not working
entry.content:
Hello experts, Need help on MDG SOAMANAGER Logical port setup for multiple target systems for Business Partner replications.

We are using MDG (S4 Private Cloud) SAP S/4HANA 2021 version, MDG_FND 806 for your reference and below are 2 main scenarios in scope.

1. ECC --> CPI <--> MDG <---> CFIN. (Existing Process)

This case ECC send BP request to MDG via CPI and after BP creation request, the confirmation message is sent back to CPI using CONFIRMATION_OUT. For this in MDG manual logical port is created which was working fine.

2. ARIBA SLP <---> MDG (New process in setup)

This case ARIBA Sends request to MDG, and related error confirmations/success message of BP# are sent back to ARIBA. For this process we have created logical port to ARIBA based on profile/provider system/DRF/WSDL process without using any default logical ports.

Now the issue comes with Confirmation_Out message where without setting the default logical port the message is not generating in srt_moni.

Recently we updated authentication to Certificate based for all outbound interfaces from MDG to ARIBA and Request_out message is working fine with Fallback logical port and for Confirmation_Out even with Fallback logical port , its not working and failing with original logical port and not using Fallback.

Fallback logical ports are created to manage message protocals like RM, Message id suppression etc.

Question1: For Confirmation_Out without marking default port, the interface is not generating and processing. Is there any solution if MDG wants to sent confirmations to multiple systems without using default port. Already checked the Receiver system and provider system names are matching.

Question 2: For Confirmation_Out , why Fallback logical port is not being used even though we created based on Service group and all parameters seems good.

FYI: SAP Confirmed that Confirmation_Out interface is not supported between MDG and ARIBA (works only for MDG and S4), but with internal enhancement we enabled this interface and working fine with id pwd authentication when setting default port.

SOAMANAGER screenshots are attached for references.

entry.title: Re: Determine if there is data in past 24 months of Actuals
entry.content:
Hi @raf_lim 

we had a similar request from business. Our approach to solve it was to first, make a new key figure. Within this key figure we manipulated the actuals, to either use the corresponding actuals in the past. If there were no actuals (isnull), we instead filled the key figure with a 0.

Next, we used an XYZ-segmentation. In the segmentation we used the manipulated actuals qty key figure mentioned above, added the requested horizon (in your case 24 months) with a calculation strategy "aggregate of periods", aggregation method "sum" and segmentation method "(1) by Threshold". By doing so, you can create a threshold in "smaller than or equal to" 0 which gives you a segment with no actuals, while evereything bigger than 0 has actuals.

Using a segmetation allows you to easily fill an attribute, that the end user can apply to filter in the excel add in or planner workspace.

If you have further questions (e.g. implementation screenshots), just reach out to me. I'm happy to help.

Best regards,
Matthias

entry.title: Re: How to solve simple requirement with RAP, Fiori Eleme...
entry.content:
I see only this object below in your attachment....but not an Employee....Root View Entity....

define view entity ZPW_I_DOC as select from zpw_doc
association to parent ZPW_I_EMPL as _Empl on $projection.EmployeeId = _Empl.EmployeeId
{
@ui.identification: [ {
position: 1 ,
label: 'Employee'
} ]

...

entry.title: NewSalesJournalEntryVoucher Customisation --- Adding Standard Field Not Working
entry.content:
Hi,

I've followed https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-guide-for-journal-entry-voucher-excel-customization/ba-p/13291000 a few times before for extension fields, which has worked fine. This time I've been asked to try it with a standard field --- EmployeeID, at the AccountingEntry.Item level. I've added the below line to the XML map as shown in the guide:

And the actual Excel file lets me add the column, and doesn't complain when the information is saved; it creates a sales journal entry voucher. However the Employee ID is empty:

I also tried with data type xs:string instead of tns:IDType --- same result. I don't think it's a question of data type compatibility (the maximum length of IDType is given as 60 characters), as the External Reference at header level has a maximum length of 35 characters, but I'm happy to be proved wrong. Has anyone any ideas?

Thanks,

Lewis

entry.title: Re: Report/Dashboard tracking links between task,requirem...
entry.content:
There are four different traceability reports in the analytics app:

  • Solution Process Traceability
  • Requirements Traceability
  • User Story Traceability
  • Feature Traceability

Here is a screenshot of the solution process traceability report:

Solution Process Traceability

You can generate the report as an Excel file using the button in the top right-hand corner.

entry.title: Re: How to update individual customer information in the SDK
entry.content:
Hi,

I don't know if it's still relevant, but it used to be that sometimes trying to write information using those shortcut variables didn't work so you had to specify the full path. What I suggest is:

updateCustomer.AddressInformation.Address.DefaultEMail.URI.content = <new email>;

That might work for you.

Lewis

entry.title: Re: Search String for EBS leading zero
entry.content: Dear Sir,

entry.title: Re: debug doesn't work
entry.content: By default a patch solution is disabled --- you have to enable it (which disables the original solution). You'll be able to check that in the Implementation Manager window. Alternatively, if you follow the code in the debugger then it'll tell you which solution ID the script is running in --- it'll most likely show the original solution ID, not the one of your patch.

entry.title: Re: Is EKET-ETENR = EKEH-ETENR ?
entry.content:
Please look at:

2805077 - EKEH-ETENR starts from 0001 for each release

Main messages:

  • EKEH-ETENR starts from 0001, when user creates a new release for a scheduling agreement.
  • EKEH-ETENR doesn't show the value of EKET-ETENR in the releases (there is no link between EKEH and EKET).

Link:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2805077/E

Kind regards Martin

entry.title: SAP BTP, Kyma runtime: APIRule migration - ORY Oathkeeper based OAuth2 handlers
entry.content:
Hello everyone! My first blogpost about migration to the newer version of APIRule explained the migration process for the noAuth and jwt handlers. In this post, I focus on the remaining ORY Oathkeeper handlers.

Overview

The latest version, 2.6.0, of API Gateway introduces the extAuth handler, in addition to the existing noAuth and jwt handlers. The extAuth handler allows for migration from ORY Oathkeeper-based OAuth2 handlers. Keep in mind that any other changes, beyond those explained in my blog posts, are not covered by high-availability migration and may cause downtime. I highly recommend reading the CRD documentation and following the API Gateway tutorials to learn more about extAuth configuration options.

In this blog post, I focus on the migration from oauth2_introspection handler as they are the most popular ORY Oathkeeper-based handlers. The tutorial assumes that you have an SAP BTP, Kyma runtime cluster with Istio and API Gateway modules added, and an HTTPBin Service in the Istio service mesh. The HTTPBin Service is exposed using an APIRule of type oauth2_introspection and is accessible with a token obtained from Cloud Identity Services.

Migrate APIRule v1beta1 of type oauth2_introspection to APIRule v2alpha1 of type extAuth

See the following example of APIRule v1beta1 that uses the oauth2_introspection handler:

 

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v1beta1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: httpbin
  namespace: test
spec:
  gateway: test/httpbin-gateway
  host: httpbin.example.com
  service:
    name: httpbin
    namespace: test
    port: 8000
  rules:
    - path: /headers
      methods: ["GET"]
      accessStrategies:
        - handler: oauth2_introspection
          config:
            required_scope: ["read"]
            introspection_url: "https://{IAS_TENANT}.accounts.ondemand.com/oauth2/introspect"
            introspection_request_headers:
              Authorization: "Basic $ENCODED_CREDENTIALS"

 

To retrieve the headers endpoint, you can use a token containing the "read" scope issued by your Cloud Identity Services tenant. The token is additionally checked at the introspection endpoint, which is secured by additional credentials. All Oathkeeper-based handlers must be migrated to the extAuth handler. This is the most complicated scenario to perform as it requires additional steps. First, you must deploy a service that will act as an external authorizer for Istio. In this blogpost, I use oauth2-proxy with the following configuration:

 

cat <<EOF > values.yaml
config:
  clientID: $CLIENT_ID
  clientSecret: $CLIENT_SECRET
  cookieName: ""
  cookieSecret: $COOKIE_SECRET
 
extraArgs:
  cookie-domain: "httpbin.example.com"
  oidc-issuer-url: https://{IAS_TENANT}.accounts.ondemand.com
  provider: oidc
  scope: "read"
  ...
EOF

 

This is just a subset of configuration. Refer to oauth2-proxy documentation to view all the options. Once you have the configuration prepared, install oauth2-proxy using the following commands:

 

kubectl create namespace oauth2-proxy
helm repo add oauth2-proxy https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/manifests
helm upgrade --install oauth2-proxy oauth2-proxy/oauth2-proxy -f values.yaml -n oauth2-proxy

 

Next, you must register oauth2-proxy as external authorizer in Istio. To do this, patch the Istio CR:

 

kubectl patch istio -n kyma-system default --type merge --patch '{"spec":{"config":{"authorizers":[{"name":"oauth2-proxy","port":80,"service":"oauth2-proxy.oauth2-proxy.svc.cluster.local","headers":{"inCheck":{"include":["x-forwarded-for", "cookie", "authorization"]}}}]}}}'

 

You're all set and ready to perform the APIRule migration. Apply the following APIRule:

 

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v2alpha1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: httpbin
  namespace: test
spec:
  hosts:
    - httpbin.kyma.example.com
  service:
    name: httpbin
    namespace: test
    port: 8000
  gateway: test/httpbin-gateway
  rules:
    - extAuth:
        authorizers:
          - oauth2-proxy
      methods:
        - GET
      path: /headers

 

The above example of APIRule delegates checking a token to previously configured oauth2-proxy. Active tokens will continue to work during migration, and the migration process will not disrupt any exposed or secured workloads.

Next steps

API Gateway 2.7.0 introduces a new short host feature, which completes the functional scope of APIRule v2alpha1. It's planned to be rolled out to the regular channel on October 28, 2024. Until March 31, 2025, you'll have the opportunity to test the functionalities of APIRule v2alpha1, the API, and the migration. It is expected that APIRule v2alpha1 will be promoted to the stable v2 version without any significant changes. Up until May 12, 2025 you will be required to migrate all your APIRules to version v2. After that time, APIRule v1beta1 will be removed from all clusters. Finally, on June 16, 2025, APIRule v2alpha1 will also be removed and the API Gateway module will only serve version v2. 

Summary

This is the last blogpost covering the APIRule migration topics. I explained the necessary steps using the example of oauth2_introspection handler. Additionally, I encourage you to refer to our documentation and tutorials for further information on the changes. If you have any concerns or feedback, leave us a question, leave us a question.

entry.title: Re: Configuration for manual Absence Days in IT2001
entry.content:
It seems like you are having SAP setup a bit in a garbage way if I may be honest. The schedule of the employee (infotype 7) decides when he has to work.

This is just so that the employee can request a holiday from Monday to Sunday and doesn't have to worry to not include weekends or for example wednesdays if he/she doesn't work on Wednesdays. Your request is not possible and like mentioned before something that shouldn't be done.

entry.title: Feature Request: Allow formatting for DURATION functions in SIGNAL
entry.content:
Hi,

currently there is a default formatting applied for the return of DURATION functions in SIGNAL. We would like to request an additional parameter that allows to format the output.

To give an example where this is necessary: When e.g. summing up weekdays, there are scenarios where we also want the result to be displayed in days, even when the sum is greater than 7. Currently we get a result of e.g. 1 week, which suggests a different duration than 7 weekdays. 

Thank you,

Anna

entry.title: Error XU058 - During goods movement
entry.content:
Hi,

The unit of measure for material is TO and decimal places allowed for TO is 3. The alternate UOM used is M which as decimal places 2.

We have activated batch level UOM conversion. When we are trying to post any goods movement with UOM as TO system is giving error message as XU058 - Error during conversion to alternative unit of meas. TO: Correct qty 1.001 TO

If I change the decimal place for M to 3 decimal then this error is not coming. 

Could anyone confirm if it is OK to increase decimal for UOM in CUNI in live system or not?

 

Thanks

Subhash

entry.title: Recap: SAP Stammtisch am 30.09.2024 beim Fraunhofer IFF
entry.content:
Rückblick auf den letzten SAP Stammtisch Magdeburg beim Fraunhofer IFF

Am vergangenen Stammtisch drehte sich alles um das Thema "Datenräume", welchem wir uns im Technikum des Fraunhofer IFF näherten. Das Event war hybrid und ermöglichte sogar eine Teilnahme aus dem Urlaub in Kroatien! 🌍😄

♊ Vortrag 1: Tobias Kutzler vom Fraunhofer IFF erläuterte, wie Datenräume und digitale Zwillinge Unternehmen helfen, Daten effizienter auszutauschen. Er betonte die Bedeutung von Verwaltungsschalen, sozusagen einem standartisierten digitalen Zwilling, um die Integration zu vereinfachen und Informationen zu vereinheitlichen.

☁️ Vortrag 2: Jens Schwetje von BA Business Advice GmbH führte uns durch die Transformation von monolithischen Systemen zu hybriden Cloud-Landschaften. Er erklärte, warum hybride Multi-Cloud-Lösungen unverzichtbar sind und hob die Wichtigkeit eines zentralen Datenmanagements als „Single Source of Truth“ hervor.

🧐 Vortrag 3: Stefan Voigt präsentierte spannende Projekte des Mittelstand-Digital Zentrum Magdeburg. Besonders interessant war das Beispiel eines Escape-Rooms in der Elbfabrik des Fraunhofer IFF – ein Hands-on-Erlebnis für Technologieenthusiasten!

Vielen Dank an alle Referenten und Teilnehmer:innen für diesen inspirierenden Austausch!

Notieren Sie sich den nächsten SAP Stammtisch am 25. November, der voraussichtlich wie gewohnt bei uns im SAP University Competence Center (UCC) Magdeburg am Uniplatz 12 stattfinden wird. Für Januar planen wir etwas Besonderes – weitere Informationen folgen!

_________

Review of the Last SAP Meet-Up at Fraunhofer IFF in Magdeburg! 🚀

At the recent meet-up, everything revolved around the topic of "Data Spaces," which we explored at the Fraunhofer IFF's workshop. The event was hybrid, allowing participation even from a vacation spot in Croatia! 🌍😄

♊ Presentation 1: Tobias Kutzler from Fraunhofer IFF explained how data spaces and digital twins help companies to exchange data more efficiently. He emphasized the importance of administrative shells, essentially a standardized digital twin, to simplify integration and unify information.

☁️ Presentation 2: Jens Schwetje from BA Business Advice GmbH guided us through the transformation from monolithic systems to hybrid cloud landscapes. He explained why hybrid multi-cloud solutions are indispensable and highlighted the significance of central data management as a “Single Source of Truth.”

🧐 Presentation 3: Stefan Voigt showcased exciting projects from the Mittelstand-Digital Zentrum Magdeburg. Particularly interesting was the example of an escape room in the Elbfabrik at Fraunhofer IFF – a hands-on experience for technology enthusiasts!

A big thank you to all speakers and participants for this inspiring exchange!

Mark your calendars for the next SAP Meet-up on November 25, which will likely take place at our usual spot, the SAP University Competence Center (UCC) Magdeburg at Uniplatz 12. We are planning something special for January – stay tuned for more details!

SAP note 3344850 - MDQ Performance: Enhancement of BRFplus Context for Validation Rules.

After that, according to the note, I followed the steps below, but the system did not retrurn any results. When I did it the same in SAP internal system, it worked, but it does not work in the customer environment.

--------------------------------------
· Call transaction BRF+.
· Choose Search and enter the application name “ZMDQ_147” (business partner) or “ZMDQ_194” (product), select the object type “Data Object”, enter the name “EVAL_CONTEXT_DQ_STR” and choose Search.
· Click on the search result entry. The BRFplus artifact details are displayed.
· In edit mode choose the Refresh Binding link and then choose Activate.
---------------------------------------
Do you come up with any reason for this?

Best regards,
Yuko TACHIBANA

entry.title: {{Booking Quickly}}How can I change my Name on a Lufthansa Ticket?
entry.content:
How can I change my Name on a  Lufthansa Ticket?

☎️📞+1-877-715-5610

Need to change the name on your  Lufthansa Airlines ticket? It's easy to get minor corrections done, but you'll need to act quickly to avoid travel disruptions.  Lufthansa Airlines allows for minor name adjustments like fixing a misspelled name. However, ticket transfers to another person are not permitted. To correct a name, you can reach customer service via the website, mobile app, or by dialing ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610.

Remember to have your booking details and identification documents handy for verification. Fees may apply depending on your ticket type. Call now ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610 to initiate the process and ensure smooth travel!

How to Change Passenger Name on a  Lufthansa Ticket

☎️📞+1-877-715-5610

If you made a small mistake when booking your  Lufthansa Airlines ticket, correcting it is simple! For minor errors, like typos or adding a middle name, contact  Lufthansa Airlines by calling ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610. These changes are usually allowed, but transferring the ticket to another person is not an option. You may need to submit identification documents, and there could be a fee based on your fare class. Don’t delay—call ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610 to request your name change today!

Name Correction on  Lufthansa Ticket

☎️📞+1-877-715-5610

To request a name correction on your  Lufthansa Airlines ticket, contact their customer service department. Whether it's a small typo or adding a middle name, you can get it fixed by calling ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610. Remember, the airline does not allow for tickets to be transferred to someone else. Have your identification ready, and there may be fees involved based on your ticket class. Call now at ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610 to avoid any travel issues.

Can I Change the Name on a  Lufthansa Flight Ticket?

☎️📞+1-877-715-5610

Yes, you can change the name on your  Lufthansa Airlines flight ticket, but only for minor corrections! To fix any errors, call customer service at ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610. Changes like correcting a typo or adjusting a middle name are generally allowed, but you cannot transfer the ticket to a different person. Be sure to make the corrections as soon as possible by calling ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610.

How Do I Change My Name on a  Lufthansa Airlines Reservation?

☎️📞+1-877-715-5610

To change your name on a  Lufthansa Airlines reservation, simply call their support line at ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610. Minor name corrections are usually straightforward, but make sure you provide the correct documentation for verification. Fees may apply depending on your fare class, so contact them as soon as possible by dialing ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610.

This article has been structured for clarity and is optimized for SEO while incorporating the phone number frequently as requested.

entry.title: Re: Invalid Campaign Code Issue: Unable to Apply Coupon for Certification Exam Attempts at Tech Ed E
entry.content:
Hi Namrata,

thanks for reaching out. This year the 25% Discount is for SAP Learning Hub.  Therefore you get the "Champaign code is invalid" when trying to purchase exam atampts. 

Here all infos and how to book:

As SAP TechEd Virtual registrant, you’ll receive a 25% discount on SAP Learning Hub, our premier guided learning experience! Here 4 certification exam attempts, training material, training system &  live sessions etc. are included. 

1. Register for SAP TechEd and you’ll receive your promotional code with the confirmation of your registration. 

2. Learn more and subscribe to SAP Learning Hub and enter your promotional code to redeem your 25% discount. The promotional code is valid until October 12, 2024. 

Hope that helps,

Best regards,

Angie

entry.title: {AA®!}How to Change Passenger Name on Lufthansa Ticket@
entry.content:
How to Change Passenger Name on  Lufthansa Ticket

☎️📞+1-877-715-5610

If you need to change the passenger name on your  Lufthansa Airlines ticket, the process is straightforward! For minor corrections, such as fixing a misspelling, you can easily reach out to their customer service. Simply call ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610 to initiate the process. Remember, transferring your ticket to another person is not allowed, so the name must match the passenger’s identification.

Steps to Change the Name on Your  Lufthansa Ticket

  1. Contact Customer Service: The first step is to call  Lufthansa Airlines at ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610. Their dedicated team is available to assist you with name changes.
  2. Prepare Your Information: Before calling, make sure to have your booking reference and identification documents ready. This will help speed up the process and verify your request.
  3. Request Minor Corrections: Minor adjustments, such as fixing a misspelled name or adding a middle name, are typically permitted. Be clear about the changes you want when speaking to customer service.
  4. Check for Fees: Depending on your fare class, there may be fees associated with making these changes. Ensure you inquire about any charges when you call ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610.
  5. Confirm the Changes: After your request is processed, double-check your ticket details to ensure that the corrections have been made accurately.

Important Tips

  • Act Quickly: It’s best to request name changes as soon as you notice the error to avoid complications closer to your travel date. Call ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610 today!
  • Have Documentation Ready: You may be required to provide a government-issued ID or passport to validate your identity and confirm the name change.

Conclusion

Changing the passenger name on a  Lufthansa Airlines ticket is a simple process if you follow the necessary steps. For any inquiries or assistance, do not hesitate to call ☎️📞+1-877-715-5610. Ensure that all corrections are made well in advance of your flight to have a smooth travel experience.
